sourcePrinciples: Harness Engineering: bounded mechanical correction, review/fix loops, CI/log legibility, cheap correction, and recurring cleanup
Symphony: Linear as control plane, issues as work unit, bounded orchestration, explicit trust posture, observability, and scheduler/runner/tracker-reader boundaries

childIssues.role: landing policy and branch freshness foundation
CI diagnostics and bounded flaky retry
review-comment response and draft PR readiness
merge shepherd idempotence and post-merge cleanup repair

evidence.testName: shepherds a mergeable PR from Merging to Done without running Codex
blocks merge shepherding when landing validation evidence is stale for the selected head
runs a bounded CI fixer turn for mechanical failed checks with logs
requests bounded flaky CI reruns and records the attempt
escalates failed checks without logs instead of running a CI fixer
accepts supervisor-recovered current validation evidence and records the current CI check head
permits merge after authoritative split follow-up decision with fresh validation
marks draft approved PRs ready before auto-promoting landing when configured
marks a draft pull request ready through the GitHub CLI boundary
does not merge review-only PR roles from Merging
does not run merge again when recorded merge state is already terminal
records cleanup warnings instead of retrying when local branch deletion is blocked by an AgentOS worktree
cleans local and remote same-repository AgentOS branches after merge
does not start merge shepherding when landing gates are only partially enabled
keeps default workflows from auto-promoting approved PRs toward landing
keeps the public base workflow template out of landing mode
reports protected branch / merge queue requirements as report-only during branch-update planning
reports merge conflicts as report-only during branch-update planning and does not run gh pr update-branch

knownRemainingDeviations: High-throughput landing is opt-in for trusted dogfood/internal workflows; public templates remain conservative and manual by default.
Protected branch and merge queue requirements are report-only until a future issue implements provider-specific merge-queue support.
The orchestrator still owns merge shepherd tracker transitions in orchestrator-owned mode; hybrid and agent-owned tracker writes remain separately gated.
nonMvpFutureWork: Registry-wide final MVP certification across multiple projects.
Optional HTTP dashboard and JSON API productization.
Validation/test-suite runtime optimization and cost-aware model routing.
